utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well hello christina so how are you doing i was notified you were in the hospital for some heart failure what happened

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i'm doing better now but i just started having problems that my ankles were swelling and could n't get them to go down even when i went to bed and could n't breathe very good had to get propped up in bed so i could breathe at night and so i just really got to feeling bad i called my friend diane and she said i probably ought to call nine one one since i was having a hard time talking and so i called nine one one and they sent out an ambulance and they took me into the er on the it was quite an experience

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: having an ambulance ride and and i've never done that before so not an experience i wan na do again either

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm sure you do n't yeah i see that your blood pressure was high also it was two hundred over ninety have you been

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i guess is that really high

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah that's

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i feel really bad

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah that's pretty high are you taking your medications or you missing some doses

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't know i might miss one now but i try to take them all time

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah yeah you really need to take them very consistently now you also said you were watching your diet did you did you have some slips with that you said your ankles were swelling

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i yeah i do i like to i like to eat

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: are you eating a lot of salty foods and pizza or

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i like potato chips

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i like the salt and vinegar potato chips they're really good so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well so do you do you go out to eat a lot or do you where you where where are you eating those potato chips or is that just the home snacking or

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's home snacking i buy the the the the brand name salt and vinitive because brand wo n't taste real good but the the brand names really tastes good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: oh

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i eat those probably everyday

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: goodness well you know you we need to probably stop eating those now

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah well i hate to hate to give those up but i guess i might have to

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well since you've been in the hospital and and they've helped you out with some with all that how are you feeling now

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i'm i'm doing better

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm and they

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't do n't have quite as much shortness of breath i think maybe getting up and walking a little more is helping

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and they gave you a water pill and is that is that helping is that making you pee a lot

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah i have almost incontinence so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: goodness

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes that's not very pleasant at all

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and so they added another blood pressure medication also how are you doing with that are you feeling a little bit better

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i think so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: if i can remember to take the pills

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that seems to be a sticky point

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well a a pill box or maybe setting an alarm on your phone might really help

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay i'll i'll give that a try anything that will help

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay well that's good to hear so now have you bought a blood pressure cuff to have at home now

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i already had one but i very failed if i ever used it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: got it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'll i'll try to use it everyday now

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and you might even just keep a log of what your blood pressures are and when it's up think about you know what you've eaten if you've done something different because that may help you to figure out what you need to cut back on or how you might need to change your your eating habits a little bit so

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you been short of breath or any problems sleeping since you've been home

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i've been sleeping like a log

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay good alright have you had any chest pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no chest pain

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well let's do a quick physical exam here so your vital signs your blood pressure looks pretty good today at one twenty eight over seventy two your temperature is ninety eight . seven and your heart rate is seventy two your respirations are eighteen your oxygen saturation looks pretty good at at ninety six percent okay now on your neck exam there is no jugular venous distention on your heart exam i appreciate a two over six systolic ejection murmur which i've heard before and so it's stable and your lungs are clear bilaterally and your lower extremities show just trace edema now now we since we did the echocardiogram i reviewed those results and it does show a preserved ef of fifty five percent abnormal diastolic filling and mild-to-moderate mitral regurgitation so let me tell you a little bit about my assessment and plan so for your first problem for your congestive heart failure it sounds like this was caused by dietary indiscretion and some uncontrolled hypertension so i want you to continue on your bumex two milligrams once daily continue to watch your diet and avoid salty foods might try keeping that log we talked about with your blood pressures and what you've eaten if if your blood pressure seems a little high also weigh yourself daily and call me if you gain three pounds in two days okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i also want you to see a nutritionist to give you some education about what foods you can eat okay now for your second problem for i know this sounds like this is just for you and so for your second problem for your hypertension i want you to continue on the cozaar one hundred milligrams daily continue on the norvasc five milligrams once daily also and i'm going to order a renal artery ultrasound just to be sure we're not missing anything and then like maybe you know some renal artery stenosis or something and so so for your third problem for your kidney disease i wan na get some more labs to make sure you tolerate this the new medications and then i'll see you again in three months do you have any questions

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i do n't think so not today

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright it's good to see you and i hope we'll just keep getting you feeling better

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

detail_list.summary: The patient is following up after an emergency department visit for congestive heart failure.
The patient reports a history of lower extremity edema and orthopnea prior to the emergency department visit.
The doctor notes the patient had a blood pressure of 200/90 mmHg.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking her medications consistently.
The patient admits to occasional non-adherence with her medications.
The doctor emphasizes the need for consistent medication adherence.
The doctor asks if the patient is consuming high-sodium foods.
The patient reports frequent consumption of potato chips.
The doctor advises the patient to stop eating high-sodium snacks.
The patient reports improvement in shortness of breath.
The doctor asks if the diuretic medication is causing increased urination.
The patient reports urinary incontinence associated with the diuretic.
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with her new blood pressure medication.
The doctor suggests using a pill box or phone alarm to improve medication adherence.
The doctor asks if the patient has a home blood pressure cuff.
The doctor instructs the patient to keep a log of blood pressure readings and diet.
The doctor asks about shortness of breath and sleep quality.
The patient denies sleep disturbance.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced chest pain.
The patient denies chest pain.

Physical exam findings include BP 128/72, temp 98.7, HR 72, O2 sat 96%, no JVD, stable 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, and trace lower extremity edema; echocardiogram shows 55% EF, abnormal diastolic filling, and mild-to-moderate mitral regurgitation.
Congestive heart failure
Continue Bumex 2 mg daily, Cozaar 100 mg daily, and Norvasc 5 mg daily.
Referral to nutritionist, order renal artery ultrasound, and order labs for kidney disease.
Hypertension and Kidney disease
